steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum
steps.content: The angles in any triangle sum to $180^\circ$. In a right triangle, one angle is $90^\circ$, so the other two acute angles must sum to $90^\circ$.

steps.title: Solve for a
steps.content:
Subtracting the given acute angle of $25^\circ$ from $90^\circ$:
$$a = 90^\circ - 25^\circ = 65^\circ$$
That matches Choice B — 65.
